{{Quote|We've intercepted a rebel transmission from the Agamar system before it was received by the rebel ship.|[[Admiral]] [[Kassius Konstantine]]|<ref name="The Last Battle" />}}
The '''Agamar system''', also known as the '''Mirgoshir system''', was a [[star system]] that contained the [[planet]] [[Agamar]] located in the [[Outer Rim Territories]]. Near the [[Mission to Mustafar|end]] of the [[Clone Wars]], a [[Battle of Agamar (Clone Wars)|battle]] took place between the [[Galactic Republic]] and [[Confederacy of Independent Systems|Separatist Alliance]] above Agamar, resulting in the crash of the Separatists' [[Unidentified DH-Omni Support Vessel (Agamar)|supply ship]] during the battle.

Several [[Standard year|years]] after the war's end, during the [[Galactic Civil War]], Agamar served as the site of an [[Mission to Agamar (2 BBY)|engagement]] between [[Spectres|rebel forces]] and an [[Agamar garrison|army]] of Separatist [[battle droid]]s led by [[ST-series military strategic analysis and tactics droid|super tactical droid]] [[General]] [[Kalani]]. During the battle, the rebels and battle [[droid]]s joined forces against the [[Galactic Empire]], who launched an attack on Agamar, resulting in the evacuation of both the rebels and droids.

==Description==
The Agamar system,<ref name="The Last Battle" /> also known as the Mirgoshir system,<ref name="HC 1">{{HelmetCollectionCite|1|Databank A-Z}}</ref> was a [[star system]] located in the [[Lahara sector]] of [[the galaxy]]'s [[Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="Appendix" /> Situated along the [[Braxant Run]] at grid square M-5 on the [[Standard Galactic Grid]],<ref name="TFABG" /> the system contained the [[planet]] [[Agamar]],<ref name="The Last Battle" /> which was home to [[human]]s.<ref name="SW21" />

==History==
===Clone Wars===
{{Quote|Salvage the remaining arms and withdraw the army. Bring them to Agamar.|[[Count]] [[Dooku]], to Kalani during the Onderonian Civil War|<ref name="Tipping Points">{{TCW|Tipping Points}}</ref>}}
[[File:Agamar_CIS_base.jpg|thumb|left|270px|The wreckage of a DH-Omni Support Vessel left behind from the Clone Wars.]]
During the [[Clone Wars]] between the [[Galactic Republic]] and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems]], sometime after his defeat during the [[Onderonian Civil War|civil war]] on the planet [[Onderon]], the [[ST-series military strategic analysis and tactics droid|super tactical droid]] [[General]] [[Kalani]] was ordered to withdraw what forces remained and retreat to the planet Agamar, where he was to await further orders.<ref name="Tipping Points" /> While above the planet in a [[Unidentified DH-Omni Support Vessel (Agamar)|DH-Omni Support Vessel]], Kalani and his forces were [[Battle of Agamar (Clone Wars)|engaged]] by Republic forces and crashed on the surface,<ref name="Build R2 51">{{BuildR2Cite|51|Droid Directory}}</ref> and remained stranded as the war moved toward its [[Mission to Mustafar|conclusion]].<ref name="The Last Battle" />

Sometime later, following the [[death]]s of the [[Executive Separatist Council|Separatist Council]] on the planet [[Mustafar]]<ref name="Episode III">{{Film|III}}</ref> in [[19 BBY]],<ref name="SWGA">''[[Star Wars: Galactic Atlas]]''</ref> Kalani received the order to deactivate himself and his [[droid]]s. However, the droid assumed it to be a Republic trick and refused to comply with the order, and as a result,<ref name="The Last Battle" /> Kalani and his [[Agamar garrison|garrison]]<ref name="USWNE">''[[Ultimate Star Wars, New Edition]]''</ref> of [[battle droid]]s were left on Agamar for several [[Standard year|years]].<ref name="The Last Battle" />

===Imperial Era===
[[File:Rebels_and_droids_vs_Imperials.png|thumb|right|270px|The rebels and droids battle against Imperial forces.]]
[[2 BBY|Seventeen years]] after the [[Proclamation of the New Order|transition]] of the Galactic Republic into the [[Galactic Empire]],<ref name="The Last Battle date">''[[Ultimate Star Wars, New Edition]]'' states that the events of "[[The Last Battle]]" occur seventeen [[Standard year|years]] after the end of the [[Clone Wars]], which ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Atlas]]'' dates to [[19 BBY]]. Therefore, the events of ''The Last Battle'' must take place in [[2 BBY]].</ref> during the [[Galactic Civil War]], a group of [[Spectres|rebels]] from [[Phoenix Cell]] went on a supply [[Mission to Agamar (2 BBY)|mission]] to Agamar in hopes of recovering [[proton bomb]]s. Former [[Clone Captain]] [[Rex]] and the Spectres landed on the surface and entered the wreckage of Kalani's [[Dual Hemisphere-Omni Support Vessel]], where they were captured by the [[B1-series battle droid]]s of the still active super tactical droid.<ref name="The Last Battle" />

Brought aboard the [[supply ship]]'s command [[Bridge (starship)|bridge]], the rebels were greeted by Kalani, who stated that while the Clone Wars had ended for most of the galaxy with the [[Separatist Droid Army]]'s deactivation, he and his droids remained active after Kalani had refused to follow the order. As a result, he forced the rebels to partake in a combat simulation against his [[garrison]] in order for Kalani to end the Clone Wars in a Separatist victory. However, the rebels managed to survive Kalani's waves of B1 battle droids and [[droideka]]s and made their way back up to the command bridge, where [[Padawan]] [[Ezra Bridger]] convinced both Rex and Kalani that the Clone Wars did not end in a victory for neither the Republic nor the Separatists, but a victory for the Empire.<ref name="The Last Battle" />

Amid their conversation, the Empire, which had intercepted a transmission from the [[C1-series astromech droid]] [[C1-10P|Chopper]], dispatched an [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer|''Imperial I''-class Star Destroyer]] alongside two [[Imperial Gozanti-class cruiser|''Gozanti''-class cruisers]], where they deployed several [[squadron]]s of Imperial [[stormtrooper]]s, two [[All Terrain Defense Pod]]s and two [[All Terrain Armored Transport (Prototype)|All Terrain Armored Transports]]. As a result, the rebels and Kalani joined forces against the Empire, destroying an AT-AT in the process, which gave them both an opening to flee the planet aboard [[Sheathipede-class transport shuttle|''Sheathipede''-class transport shuttles]].<ref name="The Last Battle" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Agamar system first appeared in the new ''[[Star Wars]]'' canon in "[[The Last Battle]],"<ref name="The Last Battle" /> the sixth episode of the animated television series ''[[Star Wars Rebels]]''{{'}} [[Star Wars Rebels Season Three|third season]], which aired on [[October 22]], [[2016]].<ref name="Zap2it The Last Battle">{{WebCite|url=http://tvlistings.zap2it.com/tv/star-wars-rebels-the-last-battle/EP018796240061?aid=zap2it|text=Star Wars Rebels : ''The Last Battle''|work=[http://tvlistings.zap2it.com Screener]|archivedate=20170704001134}}</ref> It was alternatively identified as the Mirgoshir system in the [[Star Wars Helmet Collection 1|first issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars Helmet Collection]]'' magazine, which was published in 2016.<ref name="HC 1" /> The system was originally introduced in the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity as the [[Mirgoshir system/Legends|Mirgoshir system]], where it first appeared in ''[[The Farlander Papers]]'', a [[1993]] strategy guide authored by [[Rusel DeMaria]] for the [[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']] video game.<ref name="The Farlander Papers">{{StoryCite|story=The Farlander Papers|book=Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)}}</ref>
